items.title: After last night’s heavy rains, 43 flood victims evacuated to two Perak relief centres
items.summary: IPOH, May 11 — A total of 43 people from 11 families have been evacuated to two relief centres (PPS) in two districts in Perak -- Larut, Matang and Selama, and Batang Padang—following floods that occurred last night. According to a statement by the District Disaster Management Committee Secretariat, the PPS at Sekolah Kebangsaan Matang Gelugor was opened at 1.30 am and is currently sheltering 37 people from nine families from Kampung Matang Pasir in the Larut, Matang and Selama district. Meanwhile, the PPS at Dewan Serbaguna Kampung Klah Baru in the Batang Padang district was opened at 10.30 pm, housing six victims from two families affected by floods in Kampung Klah Baru.

items.title: Syariah investment expert 'Tan Sri' arrives at court to face CBT charge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Tan Sri, who formerly chaired a syariah advisory council, arrived at the Kuala Lumpur Court Complex today, where he is expected to be charged with criminal breach of trust. The prominent Islamic finance figure arrived at 8.50am and entered the court complex accompanied by Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) officers. Another company director is also expected to be charged over the same offences.

items.title: Nintendo shares plunge after profit warning
items.summary: TOKYO: Nintendo shares fell nearly 10 per cent on Monday after the Japanese gaming giant warned of lower profits and said it would hike the price of its Switch 2 console. The company said Friday it expected net profit to plunge 27 per cent in the current financial year, with analysts pointing to a lacklustre line-up of new games. In early trade in Tokyo on Monday, Nintendo shares dropped as much as 9.9 per cent to 6,908 yen.

items.title: Two flung onto road, killed in fatal pile-up on Second Link
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: Two men were killed in a five-vehicle pile-up on Second Link Highway heading towards Senai yesterday morning. The victims, an Indian national, 37, and a Malaysian, 34, died at the scene in the 5.40am accident. District police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Kumarasan said the crash occurred after the car carrying the two men lost control and hit the central divider.

items.title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US–Iran peace talks falter
items.summary: BENGALURU: Gold prices fell on Monday, as a lack of progress in US–Iran peace negotiations pushed oil prices higher, fuelling concerns that elevated inflation could keep interest rates higher for longer. FUNDAMENTALS * Spot gold fell 0.6 per cent at US$4,687.49 per ounce, as of 0038 GMT. * US gold futures for June delivery lost 0.7 per cent at US$4,696.60.

items.title: Thailand's ex-PM Thaksin released early from prison
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand's former prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra was released early from prison on Monday, an AFP journalist outside the Bangkok jail said. Thaksin hugged his family members outside the jail where several hundred supporters wearing their signature red shirts had gathered, some shouting "we love Thaksin", an AFP reporter said. His Pheu Thai party, and its earlier iterations, was Thailand's most successful political party of the 21st century, with the Shinawatra family producing four prime ministers and drawing widespread support from the rural masses.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim departs Moscow after special visit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has departed for home after concluding a special visit to Moscow. According to a post on the King’s official Facebook page, His Majesty’s private aircraft departed from Vnukovo-2 International Airport at 3.30pm local time yesterday (8.30pm Malaysian time). The special invitation from Russian President Vladimir Putin reflected the Russian government’s highest recognition of Malaysia.

items.title: Modi urges fuel savings, curbs on gold purchases and foreign travel amid Middle East conflict
items.summary: HYDERABAD: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi called on citizens to save fuel and curb gold purchases and foreign travel while highlighting the impact of the Middle East conflict on India. Appealing to people's patriotism, Modi stressed that foreign exchange must be conserved in the national interest. A number of schemes adopted during the Covid-19 pandemic can be restarted to save fuel, the prime minister said.

items.title: UK, France to host defence ministers meeting on Hormuz
items.summary: LONDON: The UK and France will on Tuesday host a multinational meeting of defence ministers on military plans to restore trade flows through the Strait of Hormuz, the British government said. "The Defence Secretary John Healey will co-chair a meeting of over 40 nations, alongside his French counterpart, Minister Catherine Vautrin, for the multinational mission's first Defence Minister's meeting," a British defence ministry statement said Sunday. "We are turning diplomatic agreement into practical military plans to restore confidence for shipping through the Strait of Hormuz," Healey said.

items.title: #NSTviral: Riders hailed for helping clear fallen tree along Sungai Besi route
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dozens of motorcyclists have been praised online after stopping mid-journey to help clear a fallen tree that caused a massive traffic congestion towards Sungai Besi. In a video posted on Threads by user @fairuzmustaffa, the motorcyclists were seen stopping and getting off their vehicles to remove branches blocking the road. Many motorcyclists stopped to help clear it," the user wrote.

items.title: PKR student wing: 'No confidence' claim against Anwar is false, Facebook post unauthorised
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PKR student wing secretary-general Aqeef Salih Salihamzul Rizal has denied claims that its leaders had lost confidence in party president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, saying the statement shared on their Facebook page was never discussed, agreed upon or decided through their official channels. Aqeef said the statement did not reflect Mahasiswa Keadilan Malaysia's (MKM) official position and was instead done by several people within the central leadership who had their own agenda. "As the student wing of PKR, MKM will always respect the institution of the president and fully support the leadership of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim."

items.title: Two-hour downpour floods two Kedah villages, 33 evacuated
items.summary: KULIM: A two-hour downpour forced the evacuation of 33 people from 10 families after two villages here were flooded late last night. The victims, including 10 children, an infant, seven senior citizens, and a disabled woman, were moved to a temporary relief centre (PPS) in Lunas. Kulim Civil Defence Force officer Captain (PA) Amirul Aliff Ahmad said heavy rain, which began yesterday evening, caused rivers to overflow into low-lying areas, affecting Kampung Sungai Seluang Bawah and Kampung Batu 7.

items.title: Iran war 'not over' until uranium removed: Netanyahu
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium must be "taken out" before the US-Israeli war against Iran can be considered over, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told an American broadcaster Sunday. "It's not over, because there's still nuclear material – enriched uranium – that has to be taken out of Iran. Netanyahu said US President Donald Trump had a similar position.

items.title: French evacuee from hantavirus-hit cruise shows symptoms: PM
items.summary: PARIS: One of five French people flown back to France Sunday from the cruise ship struck by a deadly hantavirus outbreak is showing symptoms of the illness, French Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu said. Lecornu also said he would issue a decree later Sunday authorising appropriate isolation measures being put in place to protect the public. A jet carrying the five evacuated French cruise ship passengers landed a little before 4.30pm (1430 GMT) Sunday at Le Bourget airport, north of Paris, AFP journalists saw.

items.title: Ringgit opens higher against US dollar amid ongoing Middle Asia peace talks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened higher against the US dollar on Monday, as investors kept an eye on the ongoing peace-talk negotiations over the Middle East crisis, an analyst said. At 8am, the local unit rose to 3.9170/9230 against the greenback, compared with Friday's close of 3.9185/9230. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said market sentiment continued to revolve around the war in Iran, as the ongoing peace negotiations appeared elusive amid inconsistent reports on their progress.

items.title: Oil jumps as US and Iran disagree on peace proposal
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Oil prices jumped US$3 a barrel on Monday as the United States and Iran failed to agree to a peace proposal drafted by Washington while the Strait of Hormuz remained largely closed, keeping global energy supplies tight. Brent crude futures climbed US$3.18 or 3.14 per cent to US$104.47 a barrel by 2336 GMT, extending a 1.23 per cent gain on Friday. US West Texas Intermediate was at US$98.51 a barrel, up US$3.09, or 3.24 per cent, after settling 0.64 per cent higher in the previous session.

items.title: Former aide to Teresa Kok arrives at court to face charges over RM1.5 mil school smartboard
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A former aide to Seputeh member of parliament Teresa Kok arrived at the Sessions Court here today to face charges linked to an alleged smartboard procurement scandal. He arrived at 7.30am accompanied by his lawyers. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Dollar strengthens as Trump says Iran peace offer 'unacceptable'
items.summary: SINGAPORE: The dollar advanced against its major peers in early Asia trade on Monday, supported by strong US jobs data released late last week and as the US-Iran ceasefire hung by a thread, boosting demand for the safe-haven currency. The euro was down 0.2 per cent at US$1.1767, the yen slipped 0.1 per cent to 156.905 yen per dollar and the British pound was 0.3 per cent lower at US$1.3597. The risk-sensitive Australian dollar slipped 0.2 per cent to US$0.7234, while its kiwi counterpart weakened 0.3 per cent to US$0.5948.

items.title: WHO chief warns laxer US hantavirus quarantine rules 'may have risks'
items.summary: GRANADILLA DE ABONA, Spain: World Health Organization chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus on Sunday said the United States' more flexible rules for citizens returning from a cruise ship hit by hantavirus "may have risks." "Our advice is clear, starting from May 10, 42 days (of quarantine) with active follow-up," Tedros told reporters on the Spanish island of Tenerife when asked about US policy. "That may have risks, but of course we don't force" guidance, he said.--AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Plane with UK citizens from hantavirus ship lands in Manchester
items.summary: LONDON: A plane carrying 20 UK citizens who were passengers on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak landed in Manchester, northwest England, on Sunday, television pictures showed. The flight arrived at the city's airport from Tenerife, where the MV Hondius is moored. According to information from the Spanish government, 22 British nationals were evacuated from the MV Hondius earlier.

items.title: #NSTviral: Cleaner's boiled egg gift moves woman to tears
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A simple gift of a boiled egg from a cleaner moved a woman to tears after she realised it was his way of repaying a small act of kindness she once showed him. In a social media post, user @angelinaksjean shared a photo of a boiled egg wrapped in tissue, which was given to her by a cleaner at her office. "The cleaner gave me a boiled egg because I once treated him to food.

items.title: PR: A moment of truth, only with ethics
items.summary: ETHICS is not a soft option in public relations. Yet in too many boardrooms, PR still behaves like a polite follower instead of an ethical leader. PR's uncomfortable question If public relations is about building and maintaining relationships with stakeholders, then ethics is not a side topic.

items.title: Sand Wars 2.0: The hidden geography of power in Southeast Asia
items.summary: IN the hierarchy of strategic resources, sand is rarely mentioned. In Southeast Asia alone, the scale of land reclamation and construction demand has made sand one of the region's most quietly contested resources. Sand is not globally scarce.

items.title: BMM builds talent pipeline to support Malaysia's push in global MRO market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Base Maintenance Malaysia Sdn Bhd (BMM) is ramping up hiring of local talent as Malaysia's deep pool of technical experts and expanding aerospace ecosystem position the country to capture a large share of global maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) demand. BMM chief executive officer Lee Yang Loong said the country is well placed to benefit from structural shifts in the global aviation sector particularly amid a shortage of skilled engineers and technicians. Lee said the long-term growth of Malaysia's MRO sector will depend on the industry's ability to collectively attract and retain talent particularly as competition intensifies across sectors.

items.title: New JB transit push key to unlocking JS-SEZ potential
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor's proposed automated people mover (APM) and elevated autonomous rapid transit (e-ART) system will serve as a critical economic and social catalyst for the state, particularly in supporting the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ). It is a strategic economic and social enabler for Johor," he said. //ENDS #kw Johor transit system, E-ART Johor Baru, RTS Link Johor, JS-SEZ, Johor Singapore Special Economic Zone, Johor congestion, IMBRT, transit-oriented development, Johor investment, JB public transport, RTS feeder system, Iskandar Puteri transit, Tebrau connectivity, Skudai transport © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Nuclear energy back in focus
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia should revisit the idea of pursuing nuclear energy as part of its long-term energy security, economists said, as the Middle East tensions expose the country's vulnerability to global supply disruptions and rising fuel costs. They said the worsening geopolitical uncertainty and volatility in oil markets underscore the need for Malaysia to diversify its energy mix beyond conventional fossil fuels while strengthening domestic energy resilience. Economist Dr Oh Ei Sun said Malaysia should also seriously explore importing energy from the United States, now the world's largest oil exporter, while accelerating the development of green and sustainable energy sources.

items.title: E-waste system needs urgent upgrade: Shan Poornam CEO
items.summary: Shan Poornam Group chief executive officer Adi Saufi Daud said Malaysia is facing increasing pressure from rapidly growing electronic waste driven by urbanisation, rising consumption and shorter product lifecycles. Adi Saufi, who is also chief strategy officer of Cenviro Group, a Khazanah Nasional Bhd subsidiary, warned that without a strong formal system, large volumes of e-waste risk entering illegal or unregulated recycling channels, increasing environmental and safety risks. Adi Saufi said this sharp increase highlights the need for tighter governance, stronger enforcement and greater industry accountability.

items.title: Hantavirus ship evacuation nears completion as 94 flown home
items.summary: GRANADILLA DE ABONA, Spain: A complex day-long operation to repatriate occupants of a cruise ship struck by a deadly hantavirus outbreak neared completion late Sunday after 94 people of various nationalities were flown home from Spain's Canary Islands. The operation evacuated 94 people of 19 different nationalities on Sunday, Spanish Health Minister Monica Garcia announced on the island of Tenerife after what she called a "pretty intense" day. Spanish officials said the evacuation of most of the ship's nearly 150 passengers and crew, which include 23 nationalities, would continue until the final repatriation flights to Australia and the Netherlands on Monday afternoon.

items.title: Drones target Gulf vessels as Teheran warns US
items.summary: TEHERAN: Drones were launched at several targets in the Gulf on Sunday, with one hitting a freighter sailing towards Qatar, as Teheran warned the United States that it would no longer hold back from retaliatory strikes. The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations centre said the bulk carrier reported being struck by an unknown projectile. Iran's neighbour Kuwait also reported an attempted attack.

items.title: Khairy Jamaluddin: Prime minister’s post still ‘lifetime ambition’, yet no longer the obsession it once was
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — Khairy Jamaluddin still hopes to become Malaysia’s prime minister one day but stressed that he is no longer obsessed with holding the top post anymore. Khairy, a three-time Cabinet minister and former Umno youth chief, said becoming Malaysia’s prime minister has always been his “lifelong ambition” and that he used to consider not fulfilling that dream would make him a failure in life. “I always felt that I would not be a success in life if I didn’t become the prime minister,” Khairy said in an exclusive interview with Malay Mail recently.

items.title: Khairy admits he thought ‘long and hard’ about new party, but says Umno remains his ‘natural home’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — After his expulsion from Umno, Khairy Jamaluddin said he thought “long and hard” about setting up his own political party but chose not to do so for the sake of Malay unity. Khairy said launching his own party would have further fragmented the Malay voter base that was already in a four-way split involving Umno, PAS, Amanah and Pejuang. But Khairy said Umno was already present as his “natural home”.

items.title: Khairy likens Umno to a centrist party that can ‘work things out so that nobody loses’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — Although it largely champions Malay-Muslim causes, Khairy Jamaluddin said Umno naturally positions itself as a centrist party. That is why I can exist in the party, and so can Akmal and Asyraf,” he said, referring to Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh and party secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. As such, he said the “UMDAP” accusations levelled by the Opposition to portray Umno as subservient to DAP in the unity government is completely baseless.

items.title: Rising costs elsewhere, but this Perak stall in Selama keeps nasi lemak at 50 sen
items.summary: The stall owner credits the nasi lemak supplier Othman Che Mat for making it possible to keep the price at 50 sen, and enabling villagers to continue enjoying their favourite breakfast meal at an affordable cost. Othman said when he first began supplying nasi lemak to Warung Lejen, it was sold to customers at 30 sen per packet. Patrons enjoying the 50 sen nasi lemak at Warung Lejen in Selama, Perak.

items.title: War in the Middle East: Trump rejects Iran deal as Gulf tensions and drone attacks escalate
items.summary: PARIS: Here are the latest developments in the Middle East war: - TRUMP REJECTS IRAN PEACE RESPONSE - President Donald Trump said Sunday he has rejected Iran's response to a US proposal for ending the Middle East war, deeming it "totally unacceptable." "I have just read the response from Iran's so-called 'Representatives.' - IRAN SENDS RESPONSE - Iran has sent its response to a US proposal to end the war in the region via Pakistan, Iranian state media reported.

items.title: Woman's long wait ends with twin joy
items.summary: AFTER 12 years of fertility treatments and setbacks, a policewoman's long wait for motherhood ended with the birth of twins — a journey that earned her the Special Mothers Inspiration Award at the National Mothers Day celebration yesterday. Nurul Zaanizila Mohd Zain, 44, said since she and her husband began trying to conceive in 2008, her path to motherhood had been filled with emotional and physical challenges. She said despite having remained cautious throughout her pregnancy, her twins, Muhammad Aisy Noah and Nurul Raudhatul Aafiya, were born prematurely.

items.title: Israeli strikes in south Lebanon kill two paramedics linked to Hizbollah
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israel kept up strikes on south Lebanon on Sunday despite a ceasefire, Lebanese state media reported, with authorities saying two paramedics from the Hizbollah-affiliated Islamic Health Committee were killed. The state-run National News Agency (NNA) reported Israeli airstrikes on more than 20 locations, including two south Lebanon villages where the Israeli army had warned residents to evacuate, saying it would act against the Iran-backed group there. Israel has expanded its strikes on Lebanon in recent days, and the NNA also reported Israeli artillery shelling on several other locations in the south on Sunday.

items.title: Night drives in Gua Musang turn risky as stray cattle accidents keep piling up
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, May 10 — Dark roads, heavy rain and the stillness of night have become the most feared moments for some road users in this district, as the sudden appearance of stray cattle often leads to accidents. The issue of free-roaming cattle, especially along main roads in Gua Musang, is not new. It continues to haunt road users, causing injuries, vehicle damage and loss of life.

items.title: ‘If we want to stay safe, we should do nothing,’ outgoing MACC chief Azam Baki says of his 40-year corruption fight
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 10 — The leadership in enforcement agencies requires courage and a willingness to make difficult decisions without being swayed by criticism, said Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki. Azam said that over more than four decades in anti-corruption enforcement, he has come to realise that the job often leaves a person with more enemies than friends. Without it, it is better not to be a leader,” said Azam, who joined the Anti-Corruption Agency (BPR) in 1984 and has served as MACC chief since March 9, 2020.

items.title: Day 1 of cloud seeding begins in Kedah and Perlis as dam water levels drop to critical stage
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — Cloud Seeding Operations (OPA) began today in Kedah and Perlis in response to declining water levels at dams currently at alert and critical stages, said the National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma). Nadma, in a Facebook post today, said the operations were carried out on the recommendation of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to boost water levels at dams in the northern region of the peninsula. “In response to the Prime Minister’s recommendation, Nadma has coordinated the implementation of two OPA series from May 10 to 12 and May 17 to 19,” the statement said.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan sees new Undang Luak installed amid adat and constitutional tensions
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 10 — The ongoing adat and constitutional dispute in Negeri Sembilan took another turn when a retired civil servant was proclaimed as the new Datuk Klana Petra Undang Luak Sungei Ujong, today. The proclamation of Abd Rahman Limat, 70, as the 11th Undang Luak Sungei Ujong was held at an adat ceremony at Telapak Tua Kampung Chedang, Rasah here, Harian Metro reported this evening. Abd Rahman is reported to be from the Suku Biduanda Waris Klana, while his position within the lineage is Datuk Juan Waris Perut di Hulu Luak Sungei Ujong.

items.title: From screen to state power: Malaysian fans of Vijay celebrate as actor-turned-politician sworn in as Tamil Nadu chief minister
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 10 — Malaysians, particularly the Tamil community and wider Tamil diaspora, have joined in celebrating the historic swearing-in of film star-turned-politician C Joseph Vijay as the new Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu, India. Instagram user Thiagaseelan Ganeson said Vijay’s swearing-in today was very personal to him. Meanwhile, Malaysian Vijay Fans (MVF) club organised the MVF-TVK Celebration at TSR Cinemax, Shah Alam today in conjunction with Vijay’s swearing-in ceremony.

items.title: Former aide to MP, Tan Sri and company director to face corruption charges in KL court tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) will charge a former special assistant of an MP at the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court tomorrow. The MACC also informed that a Islamic finance and investment expert with the title of Tan Sri and another company director are expected to be charged at the same court tomorrow morning. Last January, MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki confirmed the arrest of a businessman with the title Tan Sri for allegedly defrauding investors and causing losses of over RM300 million in the past two years.

items.title: Umbrella and raincoat weather: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms in KL, Putrajaya and parts of Sabah, Sarawak tonight
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm, heavy rain and strong wind warning for Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and eight states expected to last till 9 pm. MetMalaysia said Perlis, Perak and Selangor as well as the Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya will be affected. The warning also includes Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Pendang, Sik and Baling in Kedah; Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Jerantut, Temerloh and Rompin in Pahang; as well as Segamat, Kluang and Mersing in Johor.

items.title: No double standards: Deputy IGP defends different bail decisions in recent fatal crash probes
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 10 — Police have rejected allegations of practising double standards in taking action over two recent fatal accident cases. Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said the allegations were unfounded as every action taken is based on evidence, legal considerations and the circumstances of each case. However, Ayob Khan said the situation differed from another accident case involving a van driver who fatally struck a child.

items.title: Shotgun, rifles, fake pistols and lots of ammo: Baling cops uncover smuggled firearms and 180 bullets at mechanic’s home
items.summary: BALING, May 10 — Police have seized a shotgun, an imitation replica pistol and more than 180 rounds of ammunition from the home of a mechanic who was previously arrested for allegedly possessing illegal firearms. Baling police chief Supt Brandon Anak Richard Joe said the two weapons were seized on May 6 following questioning of the 45-year-old suspect, who remains in police custody. “The suspect revealed the existence of the firearms that he had hidden.

items.title: Lorry held as evidence in fatal crash goes up in flames outside Kuala Pilah police HQ
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, May 10 — A lorry that was being held as evidence in a fatal accident investigation caught fire in front of the Kuala Pilah District Police Headquarters (IPD) early this morning. Kuala Pilah district police chief Supt Muhamad Mustafah Hussin said the police received information regarding the incident at 2.45 am from personnel of the Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division (BSPT) of the Kuala Pilah IPD, informing them that the lorry parked outside the police headquarters compound was on fire. He said personnel from the Kuala Pilah Fire and Rescue Department arrived at the location at 3.05 am to carry out firefighting operations and completed the task about 20 minutes later, while the cause of the fire has yet to be determined and further investigations by the fire department’s forensic team are ongoing.

items.title: Think that Google alert is real? Google Malaysia boss warns of new online scam traps
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — Malaysians have been repeatedly warned to be on high alert as scammers increasingly mimic Google alerts, search ads and mobile apps to steal passwords, banking details and one-time codes. Google Malaysia country managing director Ben King said scammers are deliberately designing messages and websites to look familiar and trustworthy, making them harder to detect. He said users should avoid clicking links in unsolicited emails and instead verify account alerts directly through official apps or websites.

items.title: Kuching mayor: Public anger understandable, but illegal to dump carcass of dog that attacked jogger into river
items.summary: KUCHING, May 10 — The disposal of the carcass of a dog reported to have attacked a woman while she was jogging at a park in Stutong last Friday into a river is an offence under local cleanliness by-laws, said Kuching South Mayor Dato Wee Hong Seng. Wee said although public anger over the incident was understandable, disposing of the carcass into a river remained against the law. “A normal dog would not behave like that, especially towards the person feeding it,” he said.

items.title: Sabah business groups back say bringing back barter trade could boost local economies
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 10 — Several of Sabah’s leading trade and maritime associations have voiced strong support for Chief Minister Datuk Seri Panglima Hajiji Noor’s proposal to revive barter trade along the state’s eastern maritime borders and northern Kudat region. “Formalising and regulating barter trade will further optimise our port facilities,” he said. KCCI Trade and Investment representative Carl V Moosom, who previously led the BIMP-EAGA Business Council Sabah, said reopening regulated barter trade routes would generate positive economic spillover effects for local districts.

items.title: AADK detects over 1,000 drug-positive cases at Malaysia border entry points since 2024
items.summary: He said in 2024, a total of 494 individuals were arrested after testing positive for drugs involving 444 men and 50 women with methamphetamine recording the highest number of cases at 148 cases, followed by ketamine (109 cases), tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) or cannabis (96 cases), ‘4-methylenedioxymethamphetamine’ (MDMA) (80 cases), benzo (62 cases), ketum (23 cases) and opiates (four cases). “Last year, a total of 415 people were arrested, involving 392 men and 23 women, with the highest drug recorded being methamphetamine in 140 cases, followed by ketamine (108 cases), THC (70 cases), benzo (59 cases), MDMA (31 cases), opiates (five cases) and ketum in two cases. “A total of 175 individuals aged 18 to 60 were arrested this year until March 31, involving 167 men and eight women, with the highest drug recorded being methamphetamine in 65 cases, followed by ketamine (43 cases), THC (32 cases), MDMA (22 cases) and ketum (13 cases),” he told reporters after the 2026 Drug-Free Aspiration Tour: Gombak-Kita Turun Padang Aspiration Tour in Kampung Kemensah, Ulu Klang, here, today.

items.title: New Zealand tourist breaks leg after being hit by motorcycle in Chow Kit jaywalking incident
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — A New Zealand tourist suffered a broken leg after he was hit by a motorcycle while allegedly jaywalking along Chow Kit Road heading towards Sogo here early this morning. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said police received a report on the accident at about 2.37 am, involving a Yamaha Y15ZR motorcycle ridden by a 26-year-old local man and a 49-year-old male tourist from New Zealand. “As a result of the accident, the motorcyclist suffered minor injuries to his hands and legs, while the pedestrian sustained a fractured right leg, injuries to the back of his head and swelling on his right hand, and is receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Kommuniti Madani must be govt’s eyes and ears at grassroots level, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — Kommuniti Madani must play an active role as the “eyes, ears, tongue, feet and hands” of the government in ensuring that every initiative and assistance reaches the targeted groups directly at the grassroots level, said Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. He said appointed Kommuniti Madani members carry a huge responsibility to be on the ground to listen to the people’s grievances and explain state and federal government policies in the simplest manner possible. “Convey the information so that immediate intervention can be carried out every week,” he said when speaking at the presentation ceremony of appointment letters for Kommuniti Madani Kuala Lumpur and Selangor here today.

items.title: Malaysia tightens border health checks as hantavirus alert raised, says Dzulkefly
items.summary: Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad said the approach is crucial as public health challenges are now global in nature, and no country is truly safe without continuous precautionary measures. In addition, border control measures at all international entry points have been tightened through health screenings and heightened preparedness at airports, seaports and major gateways by the International Entry Point Health Office. “These measures are ongoing to ensure that vessels entering the country comply with public health requirements and sanitation,” he said.

items.title: Ayob Khan reiterates no police links to Ketereh murder suspect’s family, warns against misinformation
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 10 — Deputy Inspector General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay has reiterated that claims linking the father of a suspect in the Ketereh college student murder case to the police are false. According to Harian Metro , Ayob Khan said the allegation had already been clarified by several parties. He said explanations had already been issued by the Kelantan police chief, the Kota Bharu district police chief and Bukit Aman.

items.title: PM Anwar confirms RON95 aid review, stresses income brackets must not be wrongly affected
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — The government is currently reviewing RON95 assistance for upper income earners, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today, amid growing calls for petrol subsidies to be reserved for the lower- and middle-income groups. But any recalibration of petrol aid would possibly avoid touching upper middle-income earners too, Anwar suggested. Anwar said the government has “agreed in principle” to the proposal to exclude or partially assist top income earners from getting subsidised petrol.

items.title: Abang Johari says Sarawak to acquire more federal assets to boost economy and create high‑tech jobs
items.summary: KUCHING, May 10 — Sarawak plans to take over more strategic entities and assets of the federal government to strengthen the state’s economy in line with the Post-Covid-19 Development Strategy 2030 (PCDS 2030), said Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg. He said the move was important in expanding the state’s economic dominance, thus creating more highly skilled job opportunities for Sarawakians, especially in the technology and new industry sectors. Abang Johari said Sarawak’s economic transformation is currently moving towards a high-tech economy that focuses on renewable energy, automation, artificial intelligence (AI), digitalisation and the semiconductor industry.

items.title: Teo Nie Ching encourages individuals facing social media restrictions to appeal through tribunal chaired by judge
items.summary: KULAI, May 10 — Individuals facing social media account restrictions or content removal are advised to file an appeal through the Communications, Multimedia and Online Safety Appeal Tribunal. Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said the tribunal is chaired by a High Court judge together with at least two tribunal members. “This is an ad hoc appeal tribunal that is neither chaired nor heard by officers of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), but by a High Court judge,” she told reporters after handing over aid to outstanding Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) 2025 students here today.

items.title: Addressing Indian students, Anwar says ‘stand to be corrected’ over temple row remarks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today appeared to acknowledge that some of his remarks about Hindu temples were perceived as tacit support for hostility directed at the Indian community. Speaking to Indian students at Universiti Malaya here, Anwar suggested that his call for stern enforcement against houses of worship built without permits may have been “misconstrued”, describing such interpretations as attempts to sow division. “In many Western countries, the far right is winning because they exploit racial sentiments,” Anwar said during a government programme here.

items.title: Police ramp up patrols and roadblocks in Durian Tunggal after spate of armed robberies
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, May 10 — Police will step up crime prevention patrols and roadblocks around Durian Tunggal following a recent spate of house break-ins and armed robberies in the area. Alor Gajah police chief Supt Ahmad Abu Bakar said these measures will be carried out regularly to ease residents’ fears and boost their confidence in the police’s ability to maintain public safety and order. He added that the operation involved four officers and 30 personnel from the Alor Gajah District Police Headquarters and the Durian Tunggal Police Station.

items.title: Ministers pay tribute to mothers on Mother’s Day, highlight sacrifices and strength of family institution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — Members of the Cabinet, including Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, today shared Mother’s Day greetings and messages of appreciation through their respective social media platforms to honour the sacrifices, love and dedication of mothers. “Happy Mother’s Day. “A mother’s sincere love never changes.
